signal.title: US bank deposits rose to $18.778 trln from $18.764 trln in prior week
signal.reasoning: moderate source, strong historical pattern match.

notes: Validated: weekly deposit increase signaled stabilization in funding stress narrative and matched expected financial-credit volatility/risk repricing behavior.

evidence_pack.summary: Deposit growth print aligned with easing immediate bank-run pressure while keeping credit sensitivity elevated.
sector_impact: Financials/credit-sensitive assets repriced on funding-stability signal

signal.title: Trump embraces longtime US foe Putin, doubting own intel - AP News
signal.reasoning: moderate source, strong historical pattern match.
signal.signal_briefing: Feb 2022 peace talk failures preceded the invasion — markets cratered. Defense names typically rally 2-4% on renewed conflict risk. Wheat and European gas see immediate risk premium.

notes: Validated: predicted volatility spike and risk repricing both observed. VIX rose to 19.86 (+6.6%) and pushed >20 into Mar 2 risk window; equities gapped lower at open; safe-haven flows into gold and defensive FX confirmed deleveraging/risk-off positioning. Russia-Ukraine narrative was partially eclipsed by concurrent Middle East escalation, but predicted market behavior still materialized.

evidence_pack.summary: Signal validated on volatility/risk repricing with cross-asset confirmation despite overlap with Middle East force multiplier.
drivers: Positioning deleveraging from risk-on into havens/commodities
metrics: S&P futures ~-1.0% / Nasdaq ~-1.2% at risk-off open
sector_impact: Risk-on tech under pressure
Safe havens (gold/defensive FX) bid
Defense sector mixed/weak on peace-concession interpretation

signal.title: Oil prices forecast to jump despite Opec+ pledge to raise output
signal.reasoning: high-credibility source, strong historical pattern match.
signal.signal_briefing: March 2020 oil price war crashed crude 65% in days. More recently, OPEC+ increases have pushed oil lower. Energy stocks typically underperform 1-3% on supply additions. Airlines get relief.

notes: Strongly validated: market ignored near-term OPEC+ calming narrative and repriced immediately on physical supply/transit disruption risk. Forecasted jump despite output pledge played out as predicted.

evidence_pack.summary: Supply pledge was treated as delayed/insufficient; real-time disruption risk dominated price discovery and cross-asset repricing.
drivers: OPEC+ +206k bpd seen as lagged and too small versus immediate risk
metrics: OVX and energy options tail-risk pricing elevated
sector_impact: Indices opened risk-off (S&P/Nasdaq pressure)
Energy complex strong; inflation-sensitive sectors repriced
Electricity/compute-cost sensitivity concern for render-heavy businesses
